chunks.text: Ken Goldberg is a Professor at UC Berkeley with contact information including office location at 425 Sutardja Dai Hall, phone 510-643-9565, and email goldberg@berkeley.edu. He holds dual degrees in Electrical Engineering and Economics from the University of Pennsylvania (1984) and a PhD from Carnegie Mellon University (1990). He joined UC Berkeley in 1995 and holds appointments in Industrial Engineering and Operations Research, EECS, School of Information, Art Practice, and UCSF Radiation Oncology.
chunks.question: What are Ken Goldberg's academic appointments at UC Berkeley?
chunks.answer: Multiple appointments including Industrial Engineering and EECS.

chunks.text: Ken Goldberg supervises robotics and automation research and has published over 300 peer-reviewed papers with ten U.S. patents. He served as Editor-in-Chief of IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering and co-founded several organizations including the Berkeley Center for New Media, Ambidextrous Robotics, Moxie Institute, and UC Berkeley's Art, Technology, and Culture Lecture Series.
chunks.question: What editorial role did Ken Goldberg hold?

chunks.text: Ken Goldberg's art installations have been exhibited internationally at venues like the Whitney Biennial, Pompidou Center, and ICC Tokyo. He co-wrote three Sundance documentary films: 'The Tribe', 'Yelp', and 'Connected: An Autoblogography of Love, Death, and Technology.' He is represented by the Catharine Clark Gallery in San Francisco.
chunks.question: Where are Ken Goldberg's art installations exhibited?

chunks.text: Ken Goldberg received awards including the Presidential Faculty Fellowship (1995), NSF Faculty Fellowship (1994), Joseph Engelberger Robotics Award (2000), and IEEE Fellow (2005). His education includes a PhD in Computer Science from Carnegie Mellon (1990) and BSEE/BSE from University of Pennsylvania (1984).
chunks.question: When did Ken Goldberg become an IEEE Fellow?

chunks.text: Research areas include Artificial Intelligence (deep reinforcement learning, inverse reinforcement learning), Control/Intelligent Systems/Robotics (cloud robotics, robot-assisted surgery), Human-Computer Interaction, and Art and technology. Research centers involve Berkeley AI Research Lab, Berkeley Center for New Media, CITRIS People and Robots, and Laboratory for Automation Science and Engineering.
chunks.question: What robotics areas does Ken Goldberg research?

chunks.text: Selected publications include works on robots learning from human experience, collaborative filtering for MOOCs, surgical trajectory segmentation, haptic probes for surgery, grasp planning under uncertainty, cloud robotics surveys, and optimization for medical physics. Publications span conferences like RSS, ICRA, CASE, and journals like IEEE Transactions.
chunks.question: What is a focus of Ken Goldberg's surgical research?

chunks.text: Honors and awards include National Academy of Engineering Member (2026), IEEE ICRA Best Paper Award (2025), Chancellor's Faculty Award (2018), ACM SIGMOD Best Demonstration Award (2016), IEEE GHTC Best Paper Award (2015), and RAS Distinguished Service Award (2011). Earlier awards include NSF Young Investigator (1996) and Engelberger Robotics Award (2000).
chunks.question: What major engineering honor did Ken Goldberg receive?
